title: CRANIS2 — Product & Programme Roadmap
subtitle: What we've shipped and what's left — from a launched platform to full Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) readiness

shipped.title: Platform built and launched (Beta)
shipped.sub: Live in production at cranis2.com since 30 April 2026
shipped.body:
CRANIS2 launched to production at cranis2.com on 30 April 2026 (Beta). Shipped across these areas:

  • Platform · authentication · multi-tenant organisations
  • Products · repositories · SBOM · vulnerability scanning
  • Technical File · obligations engine · Article 14 reporting · 10-year evidence Vault
  • AI Copilot suite · public API · external integrations
  • Trust Centre · software escrow · billing · affiliate programme
  • Security & PQC · GDPR — hybrid signing, hardening, data-subject rights
  • SBOM — software bill of materials
  • Article 14 — the CRA's incident-reporting duty
  • PQC — post-quantum cryptography
  • GDPR — General Data Protection Regulation
__SHIPPED_COUNTS__ Everything to the right of this station is still ahead of us.

now.title: Where we are — June 2026
now.sub: Finishing launch, laying foundations, starting go-to-market
now.body:
This is our current position. Between now and the September CRA deadline (the next station), the focus is:

  • Finish launch — DKIM, live Stripe keys, secret rotation, ICO registration
  • Corporate & legal foundation — UK NewCo, SEIS, IP assignment, trademark
  • Finance & tax — EU VAT via the One-Stop-Shop (OSS)
  • Beta programme + customer-journey UAT
  • Brand foundation · social campaign · CRA-deadline content
  • Sales plan
  • DKIM — email-authentication standard (keeps mail out of spam)
  • ICO — Information Commissioner's Office, the UK data-protection regulator
  • SEIS — Seed Enterprise Investment Scheme (UK early-investor tax relief)
  • IP — intellectual property
  • OSS — VAT One-Stop-Shop (a single EU VAT return)
  • UAT — user-acceptance testing

cra26.title: CRA main obligations apply — September 2026
cra26.sub: The immediate horizon — the next milestone after today
cra26.body:
This is the deadline on the immediate horizon. In September 2026 the Cyber Resilience Act's main manufacturer obligations begin to apply.

The core capabilities — Technical File, vulnerability handling, SBOM and Article 14 incident reporting — are already shipped, so the remaining push is launch close-out, the legal/finance foundation, and the go-to-market motion that uses this deadline as its hook.

  • SBOM — software bill of materials
  • Article 14 — the CRA's incident-reporting duty
This is the single most time-critical milestone on the roadmap, and it is only months away.

q4.title: Turn on the growth engine; meet the product-liability deadline
q4.body:
Where the two-sided market and the new statutory obligations come together.

  • Operator Supplier Dashboard + demand-side outreach — the procurement-leverage flywheel
  • Product-liability (PLD) evidence pack + DORA pack
  • CVD intake + customer-facing SBOM publication
  • Affiliate activation + accessibility to WCAG
  • PLD — Product Liability Directive (EU); classifies software as a product
  • DORA — Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU financial sector)
  • CVD — Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
  • SBOM — software bill of materials
  • WCAG — Web Content Accessibility Guidelines

q1.title: Complete the highest-priority CRA features and add single sign-on
q1.body:
Finish the highest-leverage CRA coverage and add federated sign-in.

  • Regulatory supplier-assessment auto-answer · single-incident multi-track notification · sub-processor list · substantial-modification detection · GDPR Article 30 RoPA generator
  • Single sign-on — log in with Google / GitHub / Microsoft / Apple
  • RoPA — Records of Processing Activities (required by GDPR Article 30)

q2.title: Second-tier coverage and international reach
q2.body:
• Regulator evidence portal · harmonised-standards catalogue · Notified Body workflow · DPIA generator · regulatory policy attestation
  • Multi-language support (internationalisation)
  • Service unit-test coverage to 100%
  • DPIA — Data Protection Impact Assessment

q3_27.title: CRA-completeness — first wave
q3_27.body:
The first wave of CRA-completeness items and tidy-up.

  • Annex II user pack · CSAF advisory feed · DPA generator · Open-Source-Steward workflow
  • Evidence dropbox · help-guide stub completion · major dependency upgrades
  • Commercial-offering discovery (Enterprise / Managed hosting)
  • Annex II — the CRA's user-facing information requirements
  • CSAF — Common Security Advisory Framework (machine-readable advisories)
  • DPA — Data Processing Agreement

q4_27.title: Final coverage — full CRA applicability
q4_27.body:
The last wave — reaching full CRA readiness as the regulation becomes fully applicable in December 2027.

  • EU AI Act overlap · NIS2 risk matrix · compliance calendar · multi-product policy templating
  • Per-component due-diligence · Article 14(1)(b) trigger · authorised-representative role · Module A self-assessment record
  • Supplier Trust Centre · chat-ops (Slack)
  • NIS2 — the EU Network & Information Security Directive (2nd edition)
  • Module A — the CRA's internal self-assessment conformity route
Reaching this endpoint means full CRA readiness across the product.

pld26.title: Product Liability Directive (PLD) recast
pld26.body: The Product Liability Directive recast (EU) 2024/2853 must be transposed into national law by December 2026. It classifies software as a product for liability purposes — addressed by our product-liability evidence pack.

cra27.title: CRA full applicability
cra27.body: The Cyber Resilience Act reaches full applicability in December 2027. The second- and final-tier work through 2027 targets this milestone.
